title: Durable Semantic Facts + Memory Hygiene (Phase 3)
description: Phase 3 of the chattable-team platform: add the missing semantic-facts memory type as a bi-temporal SQLite store with reconcile-on-write, give the existing lessons store lifecycle hygiene (occurrence-weighted ranking, decay, conflict-quarantine), and mine the fail-pass signal the generator-evaluator retry loop currently discards. Ships durable facts, non-rotting lessons, and compounding fix-pattern lessons — the memory substrate that Phases 4 (team generalization) and 6 (medical) build on.

clarificationQuestions.question: Storage substrate for semantic_facts — SQLite or file-based JSONL?

options.label: A
options.description: File-based JSONL, matching the existing pure/zero-dep memory substrate

options.label: B
options.description: SQLite (better-sqlite3 / node:sqlite), per the research doc

clarificationQuestions.recommendation: A (file-based) to match the pure, zero-native-dep memory substrate

clarificationQuestions.question: Reconcile-on-write — deterministic-only this phase, or include the LLM-judged ambiguity merge now?

options.label: A
options.description: Deterministic exact-match supersede only; leave a typed seam for the LLM judge

options.label: B
options.description: Full Mem0-style ADD/UPDATE/DELETE/NOOP with an LLM resolving near-matches

clarificationQuestions.recommendation: A (deterministic-only) to keep memory writes pure/replayable

clarificationQuestions.question: Phase 3 scope for fact producers — substrate + CLI only, or also auto-populate facts from runs?

options.label: A
options.description: Store + reconcile + hygiene + contrast + CLIs; auto-extraction wired later

options.label: B
options.description: Also wire an auto-producer that writes project facts from runs/chat

clarificationQuestions.recommendation: A (substrate + CLI only) for smallest blast radius

clarificationQuestions.question: Fail-pass contrast extractor — offline & deterministic in distill, or inline with an LLM?

options.label: A
options.description: Extend the pure distiller to mine fail→pass transitions; no LLM

options.label: B
options.description: Inline LLM generalizes the fix into a lesson when a sprint flips fail→pass

clarificationQuestions.recommendation: A (offline deterministic) to match existing distill discipline

resolvedClarifications.answer: B — SQLite (better-sqlite3 / node:sqlite). Introduce the project's first relational store for the facts type; aligns with Phase 6 medical (one-table-per-metric) forward pull.
B — Include the LLM-judged ambiguity merge now. Deterministic exact-match supersede for the clean case; an injected LLM FactJudge resolves ambiguous near-matches into ADD/UPDATE/DELETE/NOOP.
B — Also wire an auto-producer. A deterministic project-fact detector runs at pipeline/chat start and writes facts through reconcile, so facts populate without manual CLI.
A — Offline & deterministic. Extend the pure distiller to mine fail→pass transitions from iterationHistory + eval results; no LLM on the contrast path.

Autonomous self-approval. The four load-bearing design decisions were resolved by the user (SQLite store, LLM-judged reconcile, auto-producer, deterministic contrast). All remaining assumptions are backed by cited file evidence from the working tree.

approvalEvidence: src/state/memory.ts (lessons store, namespace mapping memoryDir, LessonEntrySchema, occurrences field)
src/orchestrator/memory/distill.ts (pure deterministic distill discipline, SENTINEL_CREATED_AT, content-hash ids)
src/orchestrator/memory/retrieve.ts (scoreRecord token-overlap ranking that ignores occurrences)
package.json (no existing SQLite/native dep; engines node>=18 → better-sqlite3 over experimental node:sqlite)

assumptions: SQLite engine is better-sqlite3 (synchronous, mature, Node>=18 compatible per package.json engines) rather than experimental node:sqlite which requires Node 22.5+. The store is hidden behind a FactStore adapter so the engine is swappable later (evidence: package.json engines '>=18.0.0'; no existing native dep).

The facts DB is a single file .bober/memory/facts.db with a 'scope' column for per-team isolation (NOT a per-namespace .db), matching the research doc's 'semantic_facts carries a scope column' store-layer isolation. Scope values reuse the existing namespace convention where ''/'programming' is the default team (evidence: src/state/memory.ts memoryDir mapping rule).

Bi-temporal model uses four columns: t_valid/t_invalid (world-time the fact was/ceased to be true) and t_created/t_invalidated (record-time it was written/superseded). Active fact = t_invalidated IS NULL. Facts are NEVER hard-deleted — only invalidated (evidence: research doc §3 Zep/Graphiti bi-temporal).

All wall-clock timestamps are injected at the CLI/caller boundary, never read inside store/distill/reconcile core logic — preserving the existing purity discipline (evidence: src/orchestrator/memory/distill.ts SENTINEL_CREATED_AT + 'CLI handler MUST overwrite').

Fact ids are deterministic content-hashes of (scope|subject|predicate|value|t_created), mirroring distill's lessonIdFromSignature, so supersession history rows stay distinct and test output is byte-stable (evidence: src/orchestrator/memory/distill.ts:88 lessonIdFromSignature).

The LLM FactJudge is an injected interface; the production impl uses createClient and honours BOBER_TEST_DETERMINISTIC=1 for offline tests, and the deterministic exact-match reconcile path requires NO LLM call (evidence: src/providers/factory.ts:172).

Reconcile 'ambiguity' is triggered deterministically (no exact scope+subject+predicate active match, but a normalized-key collision against an existing active fact); only the RESOLUTION uses the LLM. A deterministic fallback (treat as ADD) applies if the judge is unavailable.

The auto-producer is a DETERMINISTIC project-fact detector (reads package.json / config / manifests → emits facts like project.testCommand, project.buildCommand, project.packageManager). It does NOT use an LLM to produce facts; the LLM only ever appears in reconcile ambiguity (evidence: package.json scripts; bober.config.json evaluator/generator commands).

Lesson 'decay' is occurrence+recency-scored quarantine (move below-threshold/stale lessons to a QUARANTINE.md, not delete), keeping provenance and reversibility; 'conflict-quarantine' moves deterministically-detected contradictory lessons aside rather than dropping either (evidence: research doc §5 'invalidate, don't delete').

The fail→pass contrast extractor extends the existing pure distill() and emits a new 'fix-contrast' lesson category from sprints whose iterationHistory shows fail(s) then a pass; it adds no new git calls inside the pure function — any diff refs are passed in like evalResults (evidence: src/orchestrator/memory/distill.ts signal (c) iterationHistory).
outOfScope: Vector / semantic embedding search over facts or lessons (deferred per turbovec/Letta findings — FTS/exact lookups only).
GEPA offline prompt evolution and the replay regression harness (Phase 5).
Evaluator anti-degeneration guards beyond what already exists (Phase 5).
Any medical-domain schema, ingestion adapters, guardrails, or health facts (Phase 6).
Migrating existing markdown lessons into SQLite — lessons stay file-based; only the new facts type is relational.
Auto-extraction of facts via LLM from run transcripts (the auto-producer is a deterministic manifest detector only).
A network/HTTP API or MCP surface for facts — in-process store + CLI + retrieval function only.
Per-team selection of a non-default namespace from the CLI for facts (mirrors the existing '--team not yet wired' limitation in memory commands).

features.title: Bi-temporal SQLite semantic-facts store + `bober facts` CLI
features.description: A FactStore adapter over SQLite (better-sqlite3) holding the semantic_facts table with scope, subject, predicate, value, confidence, source_run_id, and bi-temporal columns. Active-fact queries, invalidate (never delete), and a `bober facts add|list|show|invalidate` CLI make durable facts inspectable from the terminal.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: When `bober facts add --scope programming --subject project --predicate testCommand --value vitest` is run, a row is inserted into .bober/memory/facts.db and `bober facts list` prints it as an active fact.

AC2: When a fact is invalidated via `bober facts invalidate <id>`, the row's t_invalidated is set (row is NOT deleted) and it no longer appears in `bober facts list` but is still retrievable via `bober facts show <id>`.

AC3: The FactStore exposes insertFact, getActiveFacts(scope, subject?, predicate?), getFact(id), and invalidateFact(id, tInvalidated) and accepts an injectable db path (':memory:' in tests); all timestamps are passed in by the caller, never read inside the store.
AC4: A unit test using an in-memory SQLite db verifies insert → query-active → invalidate → query-active-empty, and that fact ids are deterministic content-hashes.

features.title: Reconcile-on-write (deterministic exact-match + LLM-judged ambiguity)
features.description: writeFact routes through reconcile: an exact (scope,subject,predicate) active match with a new value supersedes the old fact (invalidate + insert); identical value is a NOOP; an ambiguous near-match invokes an injected LLM FactJudge returning ADD/UPDATE/DELETE/NOOP with a deterministic ADD fallback.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: When a fact (programming, patient, medication, metformin) exists active and a new (programming, patient, medication, ozempic) is written, the old fact is invalidated (t_invalid + t_invalidated set) and the new fact is the only active one — supersession, not duplication.
AC2: When the identical (scope,subject,predicate,value) is written again, reconcile returns NOOP and no new row is created.

AC3: When an ambiguous near-match is detected (no exact subject+predicate match but a normalized-key collision), the injected FactJudge is consulted; with a stub judge returning UPDATE the prior fact is superseded, and with the judge unavailable the deterministic fallback inserts as ADD.
AC4: The deterministic reconcile path (exact match + NOOP) is covered by tests that inject NO LLM, proving memory writes are pure on the clean path.

features.title: Lesson hygiene — occurrence-weighted ranking, decay, conflict-quarantine
features.description: retrieveRelevantLessons factors occurrences (and a recency proxy) into ranking instead of pure token overlap; a deterministic hygiene pass moves below-threshold/stale lessons and contradictory pairs into a QUARANTINE.md (never deletes); `bober memory prune` exposes it.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: Given two lessons with equal token-overlap to the keywords, the one with higher occurrences ranks first in retrieveRelevantLessons output (occurrences now influences ranking).

AC2: When `bober memory prune` runs, lessons scoring below the decay threshold are removed from INDEX.md and appended to .bober/memory/<ns>/QUARANTINE.md with their provenance; the original lesson .md files are retained.
AC3: When two lessons are deterministically detected as contradictory (same signature key, opposing tag), both are moved to QUARANTINE.md rather than either being dropped, and the action is reported.
AC4: Ranking and hygiene functions are pure and deterministic (no Date.now inside; any 'now' is injected) with byte-stable test output.

features.title: Fail→pass contrast extractor in distill
features.description: Extend the pure distill() to detect sprints whose iterationHistory shows one or more fail iterations followed by a pass, and emit a new 'fix-contrast' lesson category capturing which strategy/criterion flipped and the sprint provenance — mining the transition the retry loop currently discards.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: Given a contract with iterationHistory [fail, fail, pass], distill() emits a lesson with category prefix 'fix-contrast:' whose sourceEntryRefs cite the failing iterations and the passing one.
AC2: A contract that passed on the first iteration (no fail before pass) produces NO fix-contrast lesson.
AC3: distill() remains pure — no imports from ../providers, no Date.now(), no fs, no network — and its output is byte-stable across repeated calls (sorted by lessonId).
AC4: `bober memory distill` surfaces the new fix-contrast lessons in INDEX.md and `bober memory list`.

features.title: Auto-producer + fact retrieval into planner/curator context
features.description: A deterministic project-fact detector reads manifests/config and writes facts (testCommand, buildCommand, packageManager, framework) through reconcile at runPipeline and chat-session start; retrieveRelevantFacts + a bounded serializer inject active facts into planner/curator context, mirroring the lessons path.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: When runPipeline (or a chat session) starts in a project with a package.json, project facts (e.g. project/testCommand and project/buildCommand) are present and active in .bober/memory/facts.db afterward.
AC2: Re-running the pipeline does NOT duplicate project facts — unchanged values reconcile to NOOP, changed values supersede.

AC3: retrieveRelevantFacts(scope, keywords) returns scope-isolated active facts ranked deterministically, and serializeFactsForContext produces a bounded, char-capped block (mirroring serializeLessonsForPlanner).
AC4: The detector is deterministic (no LLM) and unit-tested against a fixture project directory.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: The SQLite store is hidden behind a FactStore adapter interface so the engine (better-sqlite3 now) can be swapped for node:sqlite later without touching callers.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: Inspect that reconcile, CLI, and auto-producer depend only on the FactStore interface, not better-sqlite3 directly.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: Facts are bi-temporal and never hard-deleted; supersession and invalidation only set t_invalid/t_invalidated.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: Unit test asserts an invalidated/superseded row still exists in the DB with t_invalidated set and is absent from active queries.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: distill() and the lesson ranking/hygiene functions remain PURE — no network, no Date.now(), no fs inside core logic; timestamps injected at the boundary.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: Grep the modules for Date.now/new Date()/createClient imports; confirm byte-stable repeated-call test output.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: All SQLite access uses parameterized statements (no string interpolation of user values), and scope/subject/predicate inputs that reach paths are constrained to the existing ^[a-z0-9_-]+$ guard where applicable.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: Inspect FactStore for prepared statements with bound parameters; confirm no template-literal SQL with raw inputs.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: Active-fact lookups are indexed on (scope, subject, predicate) and (scope, t_invalidated) so reconcile stays O(matches) not O(table).
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: Inspect the CREATE INDEX statements in the schema bootstrap.

integrationPoints: createClient (src/providers/factory.ts:172) for the LLM FactJudge; honours BOBER_TEST_DETERMINISTIC=1
runPipeline (src/orchestrator/pipeline.ts:1017) and src/chat/chat-session.ts for the auto-producer hook
src/cli/index.ts to register the new `facts` command and `memory prune` subcommand
techNotes.dataModel: semantic_facts(id TEXT PK, scope TEXT, subject TEXT, predicate TEXT, value TEXT, confidence REAL, source_run_id TEXT, t_valid TEXT, t_invalid TEXT NULL, t_created TEXT, t_invalidated TEXT NULL). Active fact = t_invalidated IS NULL. Single DB at .bober/memory/facts.db, isolation via scope column. Lessons remain file-based (markdown + INDEX.md) with a sibling QUARANTINE.md.
securityConsiderations: Parameterized SQLite statements only — never interpolate values into SQL
Reuse the existing ^[a-z0-9_-]+$ namespace/scope guard for any value that influences a filesystem path
LLM FactJudge must have a deterministic, side-effect-free fallback so an unavailable provider can never corrupt the store
techNotes.existingPatterns: Follow distill.ts purity discipline (content-hash ids, injected createdAt, byte-stable sorted output); follow memory.ts namespace mapping (memoryDir) and CLI handler no-throw/exitCode=1 pattern from src/cli/commands/memory.ts; mirror serializeLessonsForPlanner for serializeFactsForContext; build everything as ESM with .js import specifiers and Zod schemas as elsewhere in src/state.

metadata.riskNotes: Introduces the project's first native dependency (better-sqlite3) and the first nondeterministic (LLM) path into the memory subsystem. Risk concentrated in Sprints 1–2; mitigated by an adapter seam, deterministic exact-match core, injected/stubbable judge, and a deterministic fallback. Sprints 3–5 are additive over proven file-based patterns.
